name: Muhammad Musa
tagline: Ph.D. Computer Sciences researcher at UW-Madison applying AI/ML to security, low-resource language access, and healthcare for underserved communities.

highlights: Dean's Honor List (DHL) in all academic semesters
Top 15 Merit Scholarship Award, Sophomore and Junior year
Senior thesis: ransomware provenance analysis, published at ACM CSET '23
Key coursework: Algorithms, Databases, Data Science, Linear Algebra, Statistics, AI, ML, Deep Learning, Computer Vision, Data Mining, NLP, Network Security, Signal Processing

Coursework includes Machine Learning, Language-Based Security, Computer Vision, Privacy and Security in Data Science, Big Data Systems, Data Management, Foundation Models, AI Safety & Differential Privacy
Fall 2023 term: straight-A record in Machine Learning (COMP SCI 760), Language-Based Security (COMP SCI 839), Advanced Seminar (COMP SCI 900)
Head Graduate Teaching Assistant for CS 320 and Graduate Teaching Assistant for CS 220

bullets: Founded Pakistan's first integrated hospital & diagnostic ecosystem app, consolidating 1,200+ hospitals, 5,000+ clinics, and 2,000+ labs into a unified, FHIR/HL7-compliant digital platform

Led end-to-end product development in Flutter/Dart, PostgreSQL, and Supabase, enabling cross-platform (iOS/Android/Web) access with secure role-based authentication, ACID-compliant storage, and real-time sync
Built Electronic Health Record (EHR) modules: patient onboarding, appointment booking, lab integrations, e-prescriptions, QR-based health IDs, and secure report uploads with encryption & audit trails

Implemented AI/ML pipelines using Python, TensorFlow Lite, and generative AI models (Claude, Gemini, GPT) for doctor-patient conversation summarization, anomaly detection in labs, and proactive diagnostic recommendations
Developed backend APIs & microservices (REST + gRPC) with PostgreSQL, Supabase Realtime, and AWS S3 storage; optimized queries using GIN indices for scalability across millions of records
Integrated FHIR/HL7/DICOM standards for interoperability with hospitals, labs, and insurance systems, enabling national-scale health record exchange
Directed MVP rollout across 10 clinics and 5 labs within 4 months; achieved 70% gross margin targets with scalable SaaS monetization
Oversaw a cross-functional team of engineers, clinicians, and business partners, while personally contributing >70% of the initial codebase

experience.role: Head Graduate Teaching Assistant, CS 320: Data Science Programming II
bullets: Automated end-to-end grading pipeline for 500+ students using Python, GitLab API, and Canvas API, eliminating manual work and reducing grading turnaround time by 90%
Developed a plagiarism detection workflow leveraging MOSS, increasing academic integrity enforcement across 1000+ submissions
Leading a team of 20 TAs, coordinating assignment logistics, grading consistency, and student support for a class of 500-600 students
Designing and integrating AI-powered real-time assistance into student projects using Ollama and LLM frameworks
Improved course operations efficiency by implementing automation scripts (Python, Bash) and version-control workflows (Git/GitLab CI/CD)

experience.role: Graduate Teaching Assistant, CS 220: Data Science Programming I
bullets: Lead lab sessions and provide individual guidance to 1,100 students in Python and data manipulation, analysis, and visualization
Fostered an inclusive learning environment, supported grading, and facilitated project and quiz development
Built AI-powered architectural design tools using Python, TensorFlow, and AutoCAD API, enabling automated 3D modeling and design optimization
Developed a predictive maintenance model leveraging machine learning to anticipate structural issues, improving safety by 15% in simulated stress tests
Integrated LLMs and computer vision into BIM workflows, allowing natural language project queries and real-time anomaly detection
Engineered automated drafting and modeling pipelines using Python, AutoCAD API, and CI/CD workflows, reducing manual design effort by 40%
Developed scalable backend services in Python (Flask, FastAPI) to support design data management and API integrations
Prototyped a predictive analytics system for load calculations, improving estimation accuracy by 20% compared to traditional methods

experience.role: Head Teaching Assistant, CS 331: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence
bullets: Curated comprehensive programming assignments for 200 students spanning neural networks, Bayesian networks, reinforcement learning, and CSP solvers
Provided guidance and support to students, growing their expertise in complex AI concepts and coding proficiency

experience.role: Research Intern and Teaching Assistant, CS 3812: Introduction to Blockchain
bullets: Collaborated with LUMS faculty to architect course content for the inaugural offering of the course
Managed a diverse cohort of approximately 120 undergraduate and graduate students
Spearheaded development of curriculum materials on smart contracts, Solidity, and NFTs
Led/built an NFT-based certification system on the STACKS blockchain using Clarity smart contracts, improving data integrity and verification
Deployed the project using ReactJS and Next.js to mint educational certificates as NFTs
Developed responsive, performance-optimized web applications for a CPaaS platform, ensuring seamless integration with enterprise services
Integrated Stripe for seamless payments on the CPaaS platform

projects.name: Active Learning for Low-Resource Shahmukhi Punjabi NLP
projects.description: Curated the first large-scale Shahmukhi Punjabi corpus and fine-tuned a transformer model for topic classification, establishing a new NLP benchmark for an underserved language.
highlights: 44,000-point dataset curated (2,500 labelled) via translation, transliteration, and augmentation
92.9% topic-classification accuracy, a new benchmark for Shahmukhi NLP
5x reduction in labeled-data requirements via active learning

projects.name: LLM Jailbreaking & Security (TAP extension)
projects.description: Extended the Tree of Attacks with Pruning (TAP) jailbreaking framework with Dr. Somesh Jha, studying how fine-tuning degrades LLM safety alignment.
highlights: Boosted jailbreak success rate from 4% to 68% with fewer queries than GPT-4
Analyzed safety-alignment degradation from fine-tuning on open-source LLMs

projects.name: Ransomware Provenance Analysis
projects.description: Senior thesis project with Dr. Ashish Gehani and Dr. Maneesh (SRI International) and Dr. Fareed Zaffar (LUMS), building an automated pipeline for large-scale ransomware behavioral analysis. Published at ACM CSET '23.
highlights: Published in ACM CSET '23
Engineered a large-scale ransomware provenance dataset from thousands of MalwareBazaar samples
Automated end-to-end pipeline: detonation, real-time monitoring, XML generation for provenance graph analysis

projects.name: WUMI Health
projects.description: Founded and led product/engineering for Pakistan's first integrated hospital and diagnostic ecosystem app, unifying hospitals, clinics, and labs onto one FHIR/HL7-compliant platform.
highlights: Consolidated 1,200+ hospitals, 5,000+ clinics, 2,000+ labs into one platform
MVP live across 10 clinics and 5 labs within 4 months
Targeted 70% gross margin with a scalable SaaS model
Personally contributed over 70% of the initial codebase

projects.name: House Boundary Detection
projects.description: Applied semantic segmentation to detect house boundaries in low-resolution satellite imagery of Pakistani urban areas, under Dr. Murtaza Taj (LUMS).
highlights: High-precision boundary detection on Multi-temporal Urban Development SpaceNet dataset
Secured funding for follow-on research based on high-impact results

projects.name: Urban Sentiment & Online Extremism Detection
projects.description: Built a context-aware framework for extremist content detection via urban sentiment analysis on Twitter, under Dr. Momin Ayub and Dr. Usama. Poster at ASONAM '22.
highlights: Poster published at IEEE/ACM ASONAM '22
Combined data scraping, topic modelling, and geospatial visualization for policy-relevant insight

projects.name: Adversarial Attack on Sentiment Classifier
projects.description: Built a baseline CNN/LSTM sentiment classifier and demonstrated its fragility to minimal adversarial token substitution.
highlights: 90% baseline test accuracy on 50,000 processed reviews
Reduced model accuracy by 50% by replacing just 3 tokens per input

projects.name: Real-Time Mask Detector
projects.description: Multi-camera real-time face mask compliance detection system using YOLOv5 and homographic view fusion.
highlights: Real-time detection of mask SOP violations
Fused 3 camera angles into a single top-view projection for robust coverage

projects.name: TUBITAK TEKNOFEST UAV
projects.description: Led the electronics and systems sub-team designing a fixed-wing UAV for an international robotics competition.
highlights: Led a team of 7
Selected among the top 20 teams globally

projects.name: NFT Certification System (STACKS Blockchain)
projects.description: Built a decentralized system to mint verifiable educational certificates as NFTs on the STACKS blockchain.
highlights: Deployed smart contracts written in Clarity
Improved data integrity and verifiability of educational credentials

research.title: Ransomware Provenance Analysis (undergraduate senior thesis)
research.venue: ACM CSET '23 (ACM Workshop on Cyber Security Experimentation and Test)
research.description: Automated end-to-end pipeline for ransomware behavioral detection using provenance graphs (SPADE framework), built with Dr. Ashish Gehani and Dr. Maneesh of SRI International and Dr. Fareed Zaffar of LUMS.

research.title: Urban Sentiment Analysis for Extremism Detection
research.venue: ASONAM '22 (IEEE/ACM International Conference on Advances in Social Networks Analysis and Mining) — poster
research.description: Context-aware framework for extremist content detection via urban sentiment analysis on Twitter, advised by Dr. Momin Ayub and Dr. Usama.

research.title: Data Creation and Fine-Tuning using Active Learning for Low-Resource Languages
research.venue: Paper in works (unpublished as of source material)
research.description: First large-scale Shahmukhi Punjabi NLP corpus with active-learning-driven fine-tuning of XLM-RoBERTa, achieving 92.9% topic-classification accuracy.

awards: Dean's Honor List (DHL), LUMS — all academic semesters
Top 15 Merit Scholarship Award, LUMS — Sophomore and Junior year
TUBITAK TEKNOFEST UAV Competition — top 20 teams globally

themes: Healthcare access and EHR interoperability for underserved regions
Public safety via extremism and ransomware detection
Structural/physical safety via predictive maintenance ML
evidence: Founded WUMI Health to bring unified, FHIR/HL7-compliant electronic health records to 1,200+ hospitals, 5,000+ clinics, and 2,000+ labs in Pakistan, using AI for diagnostic support and clinical summarization
Wrote in a cover letter to Osmo (health/olfaction AI company) that his own passion is 'applying advanced AI and ML to uncharted domains' in service of human health and wellbeing
Researched LLM jailbreaking and safety-alignment degradation under Dr. Somesh Jha, directly studying how to keep increasingly capable AI systems safe
Built the first large-scale NLP corpus and benchmark for Shahmukhi Punjabi, a widely-spoken but NLP-underserved language, explicitly to close an equity gap in language technology
Published ransomware detection research (ACM CSET '23) aimed at protecting organizations from cyberattacks
Built an extremist-content detection framework (ASONAM '22) for public-safety-relevant sentiment analysis
Developed a predictive-maintenance ML model at SAUDCONSULT explicitly to improve structural safety, not just efficiency
Coursework deliberately includes 'AI Safety & Differential Privacy' alongside core ML courses, showing sustained academic commitment to responsible AI

journey_milestones.title: Born in Pakistan
journey_milestones.subtitle: The starting point
journey_milestones.description: Muhammad Musa was born September 9, 2001. His early education and family roots in Pakistan would shape a career defined by bringing technology to underserved communities back home.

journey_milestones.title: B.Sc. Computer Science at LUMS
journey_milestones.subtitle: Dean's Honor List, every semester
journey_milestones.description: Studied Computer Science at Lahore University of Management Sciences, graduating with a 3.9/4.0 GPA and a spot on the Dean's Honor List every single semester. Led student organizations, taught AI and blockchain courses as a TA, and built his first research relationships with LUMS faculty.

journey_milestones.title: First ventures: UAVs, blockchain, and hackathons
journey_milestones.subtitle: Leading before graduating
journey_milestones.description: As an undergraduate, led a 7-person team to build a fixed-wing UAV that placed in the top 20 teams globally at TUBITAK TEKNOFEST, took 3rd place in a STACKS blockchain hackathon, and shipped an NFT-based educational certification system for KASB Securities using Clarity smart contracts.

journey_milestones.title: Research takes root
journey_milestones.subtitle: Satellite imagery, social media, and ransomware
journey_milestones.description: Built a house-boundary detection model on satellite imagery of Pakistani cities, published a sentiment-analysis poster at ASONAM '22 on urban extremism detection, and completed a senior thesis on ransomware provenance analysis with SRI International — published at ACM CSET '23.

journey_milestones.title: Moves to Madison for a Ph.D.
journey_milestones.subtitle: University of Wisconsin-Madison, Computer Sciences
journey_milestones.description: Began a Ph.D. in Computer Sciences at UW-Madison, posting a 4.0 GPA in his first semester across Machine Learning, Language-Based Security, and an advanced seminar. Immediately started teaching, guiding 1,100 students through CS 220: Data Science Programming.

journey_milestones.title: AI safety research and scaled teaching
journey_milestones.subtitle: Jailbreaking LLMs, defending against ransomware, teaching hundreds
journey_milestones.description: Extended Tree-of-Attacks-with-Pruning jailbreak research with Dr. Somesh Jha, boosting jailbreak success from 4% to 68% to study LLM safety degradation. Took an internship in Riyadh building AI-powered architectural tools at SAUDCONSULT, and became Head TA for CS 320, automating grading for 500+ students and cutting turnaround by 90%.

journey_milestones.title: Closing the language gap
journey_milestones.subtitle: First large-scale Shahmukhi Punjabi NLP benchmark
journey_milestones.description: Curated the first large-scale Shahmukhi Punjabi corpus and fine-tuned XLM-RoBERTa to 92.9% accuracy, using active learning to cut labeling costs 5x — establishing a new benchmark for a language NLP had largely ignored.

journey_milestones.title: Founds WUMI Health
journey_milestones.subtitle: Pakistan's first integrated hospital & diagnostic platform
journey_milestones.description: Stepped away from the pure research track to found and personally build WUMI Health, an AI-powered EHR platform unifying 1,200+ hospitals, 5,000+ clinics, and 2,000+ labs across Pakistan. Wrote over 70% of the codebase himself, shipped an MVP live in 10 clinics and 5 labs within 4 months, and used generative AI for clinical summarization and diagnostic support — turning years of AI-for-good research interest into a real product for underserved patients.

journey_milestones.title: Building toward what's next
journey_milestones.subtitle: Research, engineering, and impact converge
journey_milestones.description: Continuing the Ph.D. at UW-Madison while carrying forward a consistent thread across research, teaching, and entrepreneurship: using AI to serve real people — safer language models, safer buildings, safer digital records, and more equitable access to health and language technology.
